title: FILTER
summary: FILTER returns only the rows from a range that meet a condition you specify, spilling the live results into the sheet automatically as data changes.
jobUseCases: Pull all orders for a selected region onto a summary tab without manual filtering
Build a live list of employees in one department for a team report
Show only invoices over a threshold for a finance follow-up list
Create a self-updating dashboard table driven by a dropdown selection
Extract overdue support tickets matching multiple criteria for triage

examples.title: Filter rows by one condition
examples.explanation: This returns every row in A2:D100 where the Region column (C) equals South. The results spill below the formula and update instantly whenever the source data changes, with no manual AutoFilter needed.

examples.title: Two conditions with AND
examples.explanation: Multiplying conditions acts as AND, so this returns South-region rows with Sales above 50,000. Each comparison yields TRUE/FALSE (1/0), and multiplying keeps only rows where both are true.

examples.title: Either condition with OR
examples.explanation: Adding conditions acts as OR, returning rows where the region is South OR East. Use + for OR and * for AND when combining multiple include tests.

examples.title: Handle no matches gracefully
examples.explanation: The optional third argument is returned when nothing matches, so instead of a #CALC! error the cell shows 'No records found'. Here H1 holds a region picked from a dropdown, making the table interactive.

commonMistakes: Mismatching the array and include sizes (different number of rows), which throws a #VALUE! error
Omitting the if_empty argument, so an empty result shows the #CALC! error instead of a friendly message
Using AND()/OR() functions inside include; you must use * for AND and + for OR on the arrays
Letting the spill range overwrite other data; if blocked it shows #SPILL! until you clear cells below
Forgetting FILTER needs Microsoft 365 or Excel 2021; it will not work in older versions
practiceTips: Reference whole columns of an Excel Table (e.g. Table1[Region]) so FILTER auto-extends with new rows.
Combine FILTER with SORT, like =SORT(FILTER(...)), to return filtered and ordered results in one step.
Drive the criteria from a Data Validation dropdown cell to build interactive reports.
Always add an if_empty message to avoid #CALC! when no rows match.
